jokes.text: Well, uh, don't hold me to this, because I could be dead wrong, but if it's what I think it is, I haven't got a clue.
jokes.setup: Character builds up like they have insider knowledge about the meeting
jokes.punchline: Admits they have no idea what's happening
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with good comedic structure - sets up expectation then completely subverts it

jokes.text: The tires had to have treads. The brakes had to have linings.
jokes.setup: Louie blames the drivers for the company going broke
jokes.punchline: His complaints are about basic safety requirements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - blaming workers for wanting basic safety. Strong irony and character integration.

jokes.text: Anyone here go near that goose?
jokes.setup: Louie uses metaphor about killing the golden goose
jokes.punchline: Driver takes it literally and checks if anyone touched an actual goose
jokes.explanation: Good literalism joke with nice deadpan delivery, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Maybe it's time for us to shed these old, lousy, nowhere jobs and find new, lousy, nowhere jobs.
jokes.setup: Driver talks about breaking away and starting fresh
jokes.punchline: Realizes they'll just find equally bad jobs
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of optimism with perfect economy - the repetition of 'lousy, nowhere jobs' is very quotable

jokes.text: Visual: Mackenzie hanging from noose in office
jokes.setup: Louie says Mackenzie is upstairs brainstorming solutions
jokes.punchline: Cut reveals Mackenzie has hanged himself
jokes.explanation: Dark visual punchline with perfect setup/payoff structure. The contrast between Louie's optimism and the grim reality is expertly executed.

jokes.text: I wonder how long it took him to come up with that one.
jokes.setup: Seeing Mackenzie's suicide
jokes.punchline: Louie treats it like a business decision with casual timing comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie callousness - treating suicide as just another business strategy. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah, unfortunately he was the kind of guy who would shoot his accountant.
jokes.setup: Someone says Mackenzie wasn't the type to shoot himself
jokes.punchline: But he would shoot someone else instead
jokes.explanation: Solid dark callback to violent boss trope, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: 'Cause you owe 300 bucks.
jokes.setup: Tony seeks confession and moral guidance from priest about his collection job
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually there to collect money from the priest
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the entire confession scene is recontextualized with this reveal. Perfect setup/payoff with excellent economy.

jokes.text: I gave the locker room prayer for the Giants that week. The team looked so up for the game, I didn't see how they could lose. Especially with the 14-point spread.
jokes.setup: Priest explains how his gambling debt happened
jokes.punchline: Mixes religious duty with gambling logic
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - priest mixing blessing team with point spread betting

jokes.text: Father, isn't it illegal and a sin to bet with a bookmaker, even with the spread? / 14 points.
jokes.setup: Tony points out the moral problems
jokes.punchline: Priest is still fixated on the point spread
jokes.explanation: Shows priest's addiction - even when acknowledging sin, he's still thinking about the bet

jokes.text: Nah, nah, you don't understand. He only takes cash.
jokes.setup: Priest offers valuable watch from Archbishop as payment
jokes.punchline: Tony matter-of-factly explains bookmaker's payment policy
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of priest's faith in sentimental value vs. criminal business practices

jokes.text: Well, as long as you don't put your faith in the Giants again.
jokes.setup: Priest says he puts faith in the Lord
jokes.punchline: Tony warns against putting faith in football team
jokes.explanation: Nice callback tying together faith themes with the gambling problem

jokes.text: Visual: Lou-Lou hands Tony the priest's watch as payment
jokes.setup: Tony asks for his Friday payment
jokes.punchline: Gets paid with the same religious watch he tried to prevent Lou-Lou from taking
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual irony - Tony's kindness toward priest backfires as he ends up with the watch anyway

jokes.text: Splendid, Miss Nardo. I'm George Givens.
jokes.setup: Angry executive berates Elaine about missing boss Givens
jokes.punchline: Reveals he IS Givens testing her lying ability
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup with nice payoff - recontextualizes the entire interaction

jokes.text: No one quite knows who I am... Some people know my face. Some people know my name. But very few of them put them together.
jokes.setup: Givens explains his corporate survival strategy
jokes.punchline: His anonymity is so complete people can't connect his face and name
jokes.explanation: Clever concept about corporate invisibility with good character development

jokes.text: You're right, you are forgettable!
jokes.setup: Givens asks if his survival strategy isn't ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Elaine accidentally proves his point by forgetting his name mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect validation of his strategy - even Elaine who just met him can't remember his name

jokes.text: What do you think, I write those reports to be read?
jokes.setup: Elaine compliments his well-written reports
jokes.punchline: He's horrified that someone actually read them
jokes.explanation: Good corporate absurdity - writing reports specifically not to be read

jokes.text: The chair recognizes... what's-his-name.
jokes.setup: Givens stands up to give his report
jokes.punchline: Chairman literally calls him 'what's-his-name' as predicted
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that validates everything Givens said about his anonymity

jokes.text: Nuclear weapons, hospitals and T-shirts.
jokes.setup: Givens explains what the company should focus on
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of world-ending weapons, life-saving institutions, and casual clothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist list - the incongruity of nuclear weapons and t-shirts is perfectly economical and memorable

jokes.text: Well, thank you, but they're not mine... That picture came with the frame.
jokes.setup: Elaine compliments his lovely family photo
jokes.punchline: It's just the stock photo that came with the frame
jokes.explanation: Good revelation about corporate fake life - pathetic but relatable character detail

jokes.text: I call the little boy Skipper.
jokes.setup: Already revealed family photo is fake
jokes.punchline: He's named the fake family members
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - shows how deep his delusion goes with the fake family

jokes.text: Her husband drinks a little more than I'd like him to...
jokes.setup: Continuing fake family narrative about oldest daughter
jokes.punchline: Has disapproval of fake son-in-law's drinking
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the delusion with realistic family drama concerns

jokes.text: Let's break 'em.
jokes.setup: Elaine won't charge meal to company she works for, Givens says they fired her too
jokes.punchline: Sweet Elaine immediately turns vindictive
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Elaine's quick transformation from ethical to vengeful

jokes.text: What was that? / I didn't like it.
jokes.setup: Jim builds up that one thing would have kept him as door-to-door salesman
jokes.punchline: The simple reason he quit was not liking it
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectation with Jim's characteristically simple logic

jokes.text: I, uh... I'm your-name-here, but you can call me nickname.
jokes.setup: Jim doing door-to-door sales presentation
jokes.punchline: Reading directly from script including placeholder text
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim character moment - his literal mindedness leads to reading placeholder text verbatim

jokes.text: I did it, I got in!
jokes.setup: Jim successfully gets inside customer's home
jokes.punchline: Celebrates basic achievement like major victory
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jim's low confidence and celebration of minor wins

jokes.text: more than... something else.
jokes.setup: Jim lists what makes the vacuum special
jokes.punchline: Forgets third item in sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's incompetence at sales through forgetting his own pitch

jokes.text: A book? / Oh, yeah, yeah... I didn't get the vacuum job. I'm selling encyclopedias.
jokes.setup: Customer asks where to plug in vacuum, Jim produces book
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually selling encyclopedias, not vacuums
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - recontextualizes entire sequence. The audience realizes Jim was never selling vacuums.

jokes.text: Okay. 'Come on, Latka, there's a lot of tables to be cleaned.' / You know, I-I would not be offended by a tip.
jokes.setup: Latka repeats what his boss said to him
jokes.punchline: Directly asks the other characters for tip money
jokes.explanation: Good Latka character moment breaking fourth wall by asking for tips from his friends

jokes.text: Well, I would be by that one.
jokes.setup: Latka says he wouldn't be offended by a tip
jokes.punchline: Driver says he WOULD be offended by Latka's bad service
jokes.explanation: Basic insult comedy, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Well, I would be by that one.
jokes.setup: Latka says he wouldn't be offended by a tip
jokes.punchline: Friend implies the service doesn't warrant one
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback but nothing special. Functional but forgettable.
